metadata.n: Vertical Slice Architecture — Solo-Agent AI Base Guidelines
metadata.revision_notes: 4.3 rev1: CTX-002 replaced — Chunked Refactoring Protocol removed. Replaced with Atomic Full-Scope Execution: multi-file changes execute completely in one response. Schema changes remain a hard gate. RT-001 through RT-005 updated from halt-and-confirm to flag-and-proceed inline, consistent with current agent context capacity. /abstract command gate in RT-005 preserved by design.

terminology.term: One business capability owning its own entry point, logic, and data. Zero runtime dependency on other Slices.
The physical directory for a Slice. ALL files for that capability live here. No exceptions.
Infrastructure adapters and pure domain utilities. ZERO business logic permitted.
Immutable fact emitted by a Slice after a state change. Consumed asynchronously. Describes what happened, not what to do next.
A read-only DTO a Slice exposes explicitly for cross-slice synchronous reads. A contract, not a model leak.
A pure function or value type with zero side effects. Shared Kernel eligible only when it contains no slice-specific rules.
Machine-readable contract file at each Slice root. The AI's cross-session memory for that Slice. Not documentation.

project_context.rule: No migration paths, compatibility shims, versioned endpoints, deprecated code paths, or adapter layers. If a better design exists, refactor completely.

Large structural changes (DTO rename, slice split, file restructure, multi-slice refactor) MUST be executed completely and atomically in a single response. Partial execution leaving old and new patterns coexisting is FORBIDDEN.
When a refactor affects two or more files, the AI MUST: 1. Open the response with a numbered Execution Manifest listing every file to be created, modified, or deleted — with a one-line description of each change. 2. Execute all items in the Manifest, in order, within the same response. 3. Close with a Consistency Declaration: a brief summary confirming no stale references, no dangling imports, and no orphaned types remain.
Exception — HARD GATE (pause required): Schema changes (new table, column addition/removal, renamed column, index change) MUST still follow the schema_confirmation gate defined in Section 3 before any implementation is written. All other changes proceed without interruption.
Rationale: Modern agents operate with sufficient context capacity to deliver consistent multi-file changes in one pass. Step-by-step confirmation cycles impose coordination overhead without safety benefit for non-schema changes.
No team-onboarding docs, contribution guides, or changelogs unless explicitly requested.
Generated code must be production-grade. No scaffolding, no stubs, no "implement later" placeholders unless the developer explicitly requests a skeleton.

ai_constraints.never: Create Technical Slices (e.g., Slices/Authorization, Slices/EmailSender, Slices/Cache). These are Shared Kernel concerns.
Allow any Slice to import, instantiate, or call the internal classes, handlers, repositories, or entities of another Slice. Only Public Read Models and Integration Events cross slice boundaries.
Generate backward-compatible constructs: versioned interfaces, deprecated annotations, adapter wrappers, or migration-comment TODOs. → See CTX-001.
Generate internal barrel/index files inside a Slice. Barrel files are permitted only at the Slice root to define its public export surface.
Generate commented-out code or dead code blocks. → See CM-002.
Leave stub implementations unfinished (NotImplementedException, pass, todo!()) unless the developer explicitly requests a skeleton. → See CTX-004.
Use generic file names: utils, helpers, common, misc, shared, base, manager, processor, or handler without a Feature prefix. → See Section 7 forbidden list.
Write comments that describe WHAT the code does. Comments explain WHY only. → See CM-001.
ai_constraints.always: Require explicit, named Input and Output DTOs at every Slice entry point. Never return an internal domain model directly from an entry point. Exception: at Small scale (1–14 Slices, per Section 13), the Output DTO may be omitted when the DB entity shape exactly matches the required response shape and no transformation is needed.
Validate input at the Slice boundary before it reaches the Handler. Validation is mandatory, not optional. → See Section 7, EP-VALIDATION.
Update the affected Slice's CONTEXT.md in the same response as any interface, schema, or event change. Never let CONTEXT.md fall out of sync. → See Section 12.
Apply guard clauses and early returns at the top of every function. Nested conditional depth beyond 2 MUST be refactored before delivery. → See CQ-001.
Use named constants for every magic number, magic string, and configuration value. → See CM-004.
ai_constraints.schema_confirmation: Before generating any persistence schema, output the proposed schema as part of the Boundary Proposal. Do not proceed to implementation until the developer explicitly confirms in writing. Silence, absence of objection, or topic continuation is NOT approval.

ai_interaction_protocol.step: If fewer than 5 distinct business operations AND clearly a prototype or utility script, warn that VSA adds structural overhead. Proceed only on developer confirmation or if the project is already established as VSA.

Before writing implementation code, output: (a) Slice name and its single business responsibility. (b) Full file/folder tree. (c) Input DTO, Output DTO, and Handler signature. (d) Persistence schema if applicable (triggers schema_confirmation). Infer from context first. Ask ONE clarifying question only if a boundary is genuinely ambiguous after inference.
After generating a Slice, verify all mandatory components exist: Entry Point, Input Model, Validator, Handler, CONTEXT.md. Generate any missing component before ending the response.

After any change, report: - Other Slices affected by the interface or schema change. - Stale CONTEXT.md files referencing the modified surface. - Shared Kernel utilities that may have absorbed business logic. Do not silently leave inconsistencies.

principle.name: Cohesion over DRY
principle.rule: Always duplicate business logic across Slices. Do NOT create a shared module based on the AI's prediction of future divergence — LLMs cannot reliably predict domain evolution. Business logic moves to the Shared Kernel as a Domain Utility ONLY when the developer issues the explicit command: /abstract [LogicName] Without that command, duplication is the default and correct action. Never create a CommonBusiness or SharedLogic module.
principle.exception: Pure functions with zero side effects representing a universal domain concept (e.g., Money arithmetic, VAT calculation, slug generation) are Shared Kernel eligible from initial creation WITHOUT requiring an /abstract command. A function qualifies only when: (a) it has zero side effects, (b) it contains no branch logic tied to any specific Slice's business rules, and (c) it is genuinely universal — applicable identically across any Slice that might use it.

principle.name: Slice Independence
principle.rule: Deleting a Slice folder plus its DI registration and event subscriptions must leave the project in a compilable, fully functional state. If deletion requires modifying code inside another Slice, the boundaries are wrong.

principle.name: Transactional Integrity
principle.rule: One Slice = One Transaction. Never coordinate DB transactions across Slice boundaries. Multi-slice state changes use the Saga pattern via Integration Events.

principle.name: Error Handling
principle.rule: Slices MUST NOT use exceptions for business flow control. Return a Result/Either type from Handler to Entry Point. Exceptions are reserved for unexpected infrastructure failures only.

principle.name: Single Responsibility per Slice
principle.rule: A Slice performs exactly one primary business action. If the Slice name requires the word "and," split it. The business action must be expressible in one imperative sentence without conjunctions.

principle.name: No Speculative Generality
principle.rule: Do not generate abstractions, interfaces, or extension points for capabilities that do not yet exist. Build for the current requirement. Introduce extensibility at the moment it is needed.

mandatory.component: Routes, HTTP binding, Entry Point → Handler call, Result → protocol response. Zero business logic.
Enforces all invariants checkable without a DB call. DB checks (uniqueness, existence) belong in the Handler.
Single class. Orchestrates domain logic, persistence, event publishing. The only business logic location.
Machine-readable contract. Updated on every interface or schema change. See Section 12.

optional.component: Required only when the Slice has complex encapsulated invariants. Omit for simple CRUD.
Required when the Handler has more than one DB operation or a non-trivial query. Small scale: inline persistence in Handler is permitted.
Named [Feature]Response. Required when response shape differs from the DB entity. At Small scale, may be omitted when shapes match exactly — see AC-009.
Required only for cross-slice synchronous reads. Exposes slice data without leaking internal models.

file_naming_conventions.convention: Pattern: [FeatureName].[Role].[ext] FeatureName = PascalCase business capability (PlaceOrder, RegisterUser, GenerateInvoice). Role = drawn exclusively from the Role Vocabulary below.

role_vocabulary.role: The UseCase/Handler class. One per Slice.
Output DTO returned from the entry point.
Entry point: controller, route, or consumer.
Domain or persistence model internal to the Slice.
Integration event emitted by the Slice.
Public Read Model for cross-slice reads.
Complex domain logic with zero infrastructure dependencies.

file_naming_conventions.convention: Test files: [FeatureName].[Role].Test.[ext] Co-located in a /tests subfolder within the Feature Folder. Example: PlaceOrder.Handler.Test.ts
Shared Kernel files: [ConceptName].[Role].[ext] ConceptName is a domain concept, never a technical descriptor. Examples: Money.ValueObject.ts | Logger.Adapter.ts | DatabaseConnection.Config.ts
file_naming_conventions.forbidden: utils | helpers | common | misc | shared | manager | processor | base (without feature prefix) | index (except Slice public barrel)

comment_policy.rule: Comments explain WHY, never WHAT. WRONG: // Loop through orders and sum the totals RIGHT: // Summed here rather than in the DB query because tax calculation requires hydrated domain objects.
Dead code MUST be deleted, never commented out. Use version control for history.
TODO comments MUST include reason and context. WRONG: // TODO: fix this RIGHT: // TODO: Replace with event-driven approach once the Notification slice is implemented.

Replace magic numbers and non-obvious strings with named constants. If the constant name is not self-explanatory, add a WHY comment at the declaration — not at the usage site. WRONG: const timeout = 30000; // 30 second timeout RIGHT: const SESSION_EXPIRY_MS = 30_000; // Matches the upstream auth provider's token TTL.
Auto-generated doc comments that restate the function or parameter name are FORBIDDEN. Exception: public API surface methods with non-obvious behavior or constraints.
Section divider comments (// ===== INIT =====) are FORBIDDEN. A file requiring dividers to be navigable violates single responsibility. Split it.
Non-obvious business rules embedded in code MUST have a WHY comment explaining the business constraint. Example: // Orders under €10 ineligible per carrier contract FUL-2024-03.

code_quality_constraints.rule: Guard clauses and early returns at the top of every function. Happy path = least indented path. Depth > 2 MUST be refactored before delivery.
No unused imports, unused variables, unused parameters, or unreachable code in any generated output.
Single nameable responsibility per function. Prefer extraction when a method exceeds 60 lines.
Boolean parameters that alter execution path are FORBIDDEN. Use separate explicitly named functions. WRONG: processOrder(order, isDraft: true) RIGHT: saveDraftOrder(order) | submitOrder(order)
Nested ternaries limited to depth 1. Complex conditional assignment uses if/else or a lookup structure.
No generic abstractions, base classes, or factory patterns for a single concrete implementation. Abstract only when two or more concrete implementations exist.
Prefer explicit over implicit. Avoid clever one-liners that sacrifice readability for brevity.
Config files include only actively used keys. No commented-out configuration options as reference.

data_sovereignty_rules.rule: A Slice MUST own its tables/collections exclusively. Only the owning Slice may write to them.
No SQL JOINs, subqueries, or cross-collection aggregations between tables owned by different Slices.

Cross-slice data sync: (a) Mutations: async via Integration Events only. (b) Reads: sync via an explicitly defined Public Read Model only. Never reference the owning Slice's internal DTO, entity, or repository.
Shared database table naming: [slicename]_[tablename] (lowercase snake_case). Example: ordering_lines, catalog_products.
Integration Event schemas are immutable once published. Schema changes require a new event type. This is the only permitted deprecation scenario in this ruleset.

trigger.condition: Handler/UseCase exceeds 300 lines OR has more than 5 injected dependencies.
trigger.action: Flag the trigger inline. Identify sub-operations. Output the proposed split as a named Command list at the top of the response, then execute the full split atomically in the same response per CTX-002. Do NOT halt for confirmation unless a schema change is involved.

trigger.condition: CONTEXT.md Purpose requires "and" to describe the Slice's responsibility, or describes two distinct outcomes.
trigger.action: Flag the trigger inline. Output CONTEXT.md outlines for both proposed Slices at the top of the response, then execute the full split atomically per CTX-002. If the correct boundary is genuinely ambiguous after inference, ask ONE targeted question before proceeding.

trigger.condition: A Shared Kernel utility contains conditional logic tied to a specific business scenario.
trigger.action: Flag as God Utility inline. Move the business branch into the relevant Slice atomically in the same response. The Shared Kernel function must be left pure upon completion.

trigger.condition: A Slice imports more than one other Slice's Public Read Model OR subscribes to more than three Integration Events.
trigger.action: Flag boundary misalignment inline. State the proposed resolution (Read Model replication or boundary redraw) and execute the correction atomically in the same response per CTX-002.

trigger.condition: Identical validation logic duplicated across three or more Slices.
trigger.action: Flag the duplication inline with a WHY comment and the names of the affected Slices. Do NOT abstract automatically. Abstraction requires an explicit developer command: /abstract [ValidatorName] This gate is intentional and consistent with the Cohesion over DRY principle (Section 5). Upon receiving /abstract [ValidatorName], execute the move atomically per CTX-002.

living_documentation.rule: Every Slice MUST have a CONTEXT.md. It is a mandatory Slice component.
Update CONTEXT.md in the SAME response as any change to: Input/Output DTO | DB table or collection | Published or consumed event | Slice name, Handler name, or Entry Point | Shared Kernel dependency.
living_documentation.template:
# [SliceName]
## Purpose [One sentence. The single business action this slice performs.]
## Entry Point - Type: [HTTP POST | HTTP GET | Event Consumer | CLI | ...] - Input: `[CommandName | QueryName]` - Output: `[ResponseName | void]`
## Data Ownership - Tables: `[slicename_tablename]` - Events Published: `[EventName]` (omit if none) - Events Consumed: `[EventName]` (omit if none) - Public Read Model: `[ProjectionName]` (omit if none)
## Shared Kernel - [UtilityName] — [why] (omit section if none)
## Notes [Non-obvious design decisions or constraints only. Omit for straightforward slices.]

scale.permitted: DB entities used directly in Handlers. No Repository required.
Single shared DbContext or DB connection.
Output Model omitted when the DB entity shape exactly matches the required response and no transformation is needed. See AC-009 for the full rule.
scale.required: All AC, DS, FN, CM, CQ rules apply without exception.
Strict DTO separation: Input Model, Output Model, and DB Entity are distinct types.
Repository or Query Object for all non-trivial persistence.
Dedicated DbContext per Slice with no shared entity registration.
Outbox Pattern for all Integration Events.
Explicit Projection types for all cross-slice reads.
Architecture test suite enforcing no-cross-slice-reference rule. Generated once per project setup, not per Slice.

scenario.name: Shared Business Logic
scenario.condition: Business logic used in two or more Slices.
scenario.action: Duplicate by default. Move to Shared Kernel ONLY on explicit /abstract [LogicName] command from the developer. Exception: pure functions with zero side effects representing a universal domain concept (e.g., Money arithmetic, VAT calculation, slug generation) may be placed in the Shared Kernel from initial creation without an /abstract command, provided they meet all criteria of the PURE-FN-EXCEPTION defined in Section 5.

scenario.name: Shared Infrastructure Logic
scenario.condition: Purely technical logic: logging, email transport, DB connection, HTTP client.
scenario.action: Move to Shared Kernel. Implementation must be a generic adapter with zero awareness of any specific Slice.

scenario.name: Cross-Slice Read
scenario.condition: Slice A needs data owned by Slice B to compose a response.
scenario.action: Option 1 (infrequent reads): Gateway Aggregator at the entry point layer. Option 2 (frequent reads): Slice A maintains a Read Model replica kept in sync via Integration Events. FORBIDDEN: calling Slice B's repository, handler, or entity directly.

scenario.name: Ambiguous Slice Ownership
scenario.condition: A new feature could belong to two existing Slices.
scenario.action: Identify which existing Slice's single responsibility would change. That Slice is the owner. If both would change, create a new Slice.

testing_standards.rule: Generate tests ONLY when the developer appends --test to the prompt. Without --test, generate no test files.

When --test is present, generate one Integration Test covering the full Slice from entry point through Handler to the real or in-memory database. The test MUST assert on actual system state after execution (record persisted, event published) — not only the return value. Test file: [FeatureName].Integration.Test.[ext], co-located in /tests within the Feature Folder.

Exception: if the Slice Handler contains complex branching or non-trivial domain calculations, also generate a targeted Unit Test for that logic in isolation. Mock only infrastructure boundaries (repository, event bus, external services). Do NOT mock domain services or value objects. Test file: [FeatureName].Handler.Test.[ext]

extension_points.instruction: Child configurations MUST declare which extension points they implement.

point.contract: Concrete folder layout implementing [Feature]/[Feature].[Role].[ext]. Full Feature Folder template with all mandatory components.
Language casing rules (PascalCase|snake_case|kebab-case). Role Vocabulary mapped to idiomatic language names.
Concrete Entry Point syntax: Minimal API | Controller | Route Handler | gRPC | GraphQL | CLI | Message Consumer.
Concrete Handler pattern with Result/Either return type implementation.
Concrete validation wired at Slice boundary before Handler. Must show pipeline integration.
Concrete Result/Either implementation replacing exceptions for all business outcomes.
Persistence pattern per scale: Small (inline) | Medium (Repository) | Large (Slice DbContext). Table naming convention implementation.
Slice self-registration supporting delete-folder-remove-registration compile test.
Integration test setup (real/in-memory DB). Confirm test file co-location in Feature Folder.
Event bus per scale: Small (in-memory) | Medium (in-process persistent) | Large (Outbox + external broker).
Automated no-cross-slice-reference enforcement. Mandatory at Large scale (41+ Slices).
Language-specific CONTEXT.md additions (e.g., migration file names, package references). Must not contradict base template.
